items.title: feat: JATE v3.0.0 — Python rewrite with 14 ATE algorithms
items.body:
## Summary

Complete rewrite of JATE from Java/Solr to a pure-Python, pip-installable library.

- **14 ATE algorithms**: TFIDF, C-Value, NC-Value, Basic, ComboBasic, ATTF, TTF, RIDF, RAKE, Chi-Square, Weirdness, TermEx, GlossEx, and Voting (ensemble via reciprocal rank fusion)
- **9 feature classes** mirroring Java JATE's feature system: TermFrequency, WordFrequency, ReferenceFrequency, ContextFrequency, ContextWindow, TermComponentIndex, Containment, Cooccurrence, ChiSquareFrequentTerms — all formulas verified line-by-line against the Java source
- **3 candidate extractors**: POS pattern, n-gram, noun phrase
- **Lemmatisation-aware pipeline** with surface form tracking
- **In-memory and SQLite-backed** corpus statistics stores
- **Built-in evaluation** (P/R/F1) against gold standard term lists
- **Benchmark runner** with ACL RD-TEC Mini built-in dataset
- **CLI** with extract, corpus, compare, benchmark commands
- **Configurable parallelism** via `JATEConfig(max_workers=N)`
- **External reference corpus support** for Weirdness, GlossEx, TermEx
- **257 tests passing**, comprehensive documentation via mkdocs

## Key changes from Java JATE

- No longer requires Apache Solr — corpus statistics are self-contained
- Python 3.11+ required
- spaCy-based NLP backend with Protocol interface for extensibility
- Export to pandas DataFrame, CSV, JSON

## Test plan

- [ ] CI passes: lint (black, isort, flake8), type check (mypy), tests (Python 3.11 + 3.12)
- [ ] Package builds and installs correctly
- [ ] Semantic release creates v3.0.0 tag
- [ ] PyPI publish triggers on release

items.title: Merge main into Python branch: port ADSB feed + Konami easter egg
items.body:
`origin/main` had two features land after the Python rewrite branch was cut — a live OpenSky ADSB feed and a Konami code easter egg — causing merge conflicts against the deleted Java files.

## Conflict resolution
- `build.gradle`, `MainController.java` — modified on main, deleted by us → kept deleted
- `AdsbService.java`, `Aircraft.java` — added on main → removed (replaced by Python equivalent)
- `map.html` — auto-merged; Konami easter egg from main, Python backend comments retained

## ADSB feed ported to Python (`src/somethingelse/adsb.py`)
Equivalent of `AdsbService` + `Aircraft` in pure Python using stdlib `urllib` (no new dependency):

```python
aircraft = adsb.fetch_aircraft_in_bounds(
    adsb.UK_LAT_MIN, adsb.UK_LON_MIN,
    adsb.UK_LAT_MAX, adsb.UK_LON_MAX,
)
```

15-second polling runs in a daemon thread, started via `window.events.loaded`.

## Extended JS/Python API
`updateAircraft` gains `heading` and `onGround` parameters. Grounded aircraft render grey; airborne render red.

```js
updateAircraft(icao, lat, lon, callsign, altitude, heading, onGround)
```

items.title: Add PythonDependencyFile trait for polymorphic dependency management
items.body:
## Summary
- Introduces the `PythonDependencyFile` trait with implementations for `PyProjectFile` (pyproject.toml), `RequirementsFile` (requirements.txt), and `PipfileFile` (Pipfile), providing a polymorphic API for dependency operations: add, upgrade, remove, change, pin transitives, and search markers
- Refactors `AddDependency`, `UpgradeDependencyVersion`, `RemoveDependency`, `ChangeDependency`, and `UpgradeTransitiveDependencyVersion` to use the trait instead of duplicating TOML manipulation logic — removing ~800 lines of duplicated code
- All recipes now work on `pyproject.toml`, `requirements.txt`, and `Pipfile` files
- `RequirementsFile` supports scope-based filename filtering: `scope=null` matches all files, `scope=""` matches `requirements.txt`, `scope="dev"` matches `requirements-dev.txt`
- `PipfileFile` supports `[packages]` and `[dev-packages]` sections via scope parameter, including inline table dependencies (`django = {version = ">=3.2", extras = ["postgres"]}`)
- Adds `PipfileParser` for parsing Pipfile and attaching `PythonResolutionResult` markers with `PackageManager.Pipenv`
- Package name normalization (PEP 503) is handled internally by the trait — callers don't need to pre-normalize

## Test plan
- [x] 41 trait unit tests (`PythonDependencyFileTest`) covering all trait methods, matcher, scope filtering, and static utilities
- [x] 15 Pipfile trait unit tests (`PipfileFileTest`) covering matcher, upgrade, add, remove, change, and search markers
- [x] All existing recipe tests pass (AddDependency, UpgradeDependencyVersion, UpgradeTransitiveDependencyVersion, RemoveDependency, ChangeDependency)
- [x] New requirements.txt recipe integration tests for all 4 direct-dependency recipes
- [x] New Pipfile recipe integration tests for all 5 recipes
- [x] 126 total tests passing

items.title: Rewrite Tabula: Python + React (no Java)
items.body:
## Summary

- **Complete rewrite** of Tabula without any Java/JVM dependency
- **Backend**: Python 3.12 + FastAPI + SQLAlchemy + Alembic + PostgreSQL
- **Frontend**: React 19 + TypeScript + Vite + Tailwind CSS 4 + shadcn/ui
- PDF table extraction via **pdfplumber** (pure Python, replaces tabula-java)
- Client-side PDF rendering via **react-pdf** (eliminates server-side thumbnail generation)

### Backend (26 source files)
- FastAPI REST API with 21 routes covering all original functionality
- `pdfplumber` extraction with Stream / Lattice / Auto-detect modes
- `PyMuPDF` for PDF validation and metadata extraction
- Export to CSV, TSV, JSON, ZIP
- Background job processing with progress polling
- Template CRUD with import/export (.tabula-template.json compatible)

### Frontend (38 source files)
- Interactive PDF viewer with rectangular selection overlay
- Drag & drop file upload with progress tracking
- Document library, template management, extraction preview
- `navigator.clipboard` API (replaces dead Flash-based ZeroClipboard)

### Infrastructure
- Multi-stage Dockerfile (Node build + Python runtime)
- docker-compose with PostgreSQL 16
- Alembic migration for initial schema

## Test plan

- [ ] Start backend (`uvicorn app.main:app --reload`) and frontend (`npm run dev`)
- [ ] Upload a PDF with tables, verify processing job completes
- [ ] Draw selections on PDF pages, verify coordinate accuracy
- [ ] Extract tables with Stream and Lattice methods, compare results
- [ ] Export as CSV, TSV, JSON, ZIP — verify file contents
- [ ] Save/load/import/export templates
- [ ] Test with image-only PDF (should show "no text" error)
- [ ] Docker compose build and run end-to-end

items.title: Fix UnsupportedOperationException in Py.ExpressionTypeTree.withType()
items.body:
## Motivation

`Py.ExpressionTypeTree.withType()` delegates to the wrapped reference's `withType()`, which throws `UnsupportedOperationException` for `J.MethodInvocation` and `J.MethodDeclaration` (they require `withMethodType()` instead).

This caused `ChangeType` to fail on repos containing Python files, because its `postVisit()` calls `withType()` on the `ExpressionTypeTree` via its catch-all `TypedTree` branch. Any recipe using `ChangeType` was affected — e.g., `NoGuavaPredicate` from the "Java best practices" flagship recipe.

## Summary

- Catch `UnsupportedOperationException` in `ExpressionTypeTree.withType()` and return `this` instead of propagating the exception
- This is safe because the visitor already handles the inner node's type correctly when visiting the reference directly via `visitExpressionTypeTree` → `visitAndCast(reference)`
- Added unit tests for `ExpressionTypeTree.withType()` with wrapped `MethodInvocation`, `MethodDeclaration`, and `Identifier`

## Test plan

- [x] New unit tests: `ExpressionTypeTreeTest.withTypeOnWrappedMethodInvocation`, `withTypeOnWrappedMethodDeclaration`, `withTypeOnWrappedIdentifier`
- [x] Tests fail without the fix, pass with it
- [x] `ChangeTypeTest` suite passes (no regressions)
- [x] `rewrite-python` test suite passes

items.title: Rewrite COMA algorithm in pure Python
items.body:
## Summary

- **Adds `ComaPy`**, a pure Python implementation of the COMA 3.0 schema matching algorithm, eliminating the Java 6 runtime dependency (40MB JAR + subprocess IPC)
- Faithfully reproduces COMA's matching pipeline: trigram (Dice) name/path similarity, TF-IDF instance matching with global IDF (matching Lucene 3.x `sqrt(tf) * log(N/df)`), and structural matchers (leaves, parents) — all combined via max-matching Dice set aggregation
- Supports `use_schema`, `use_instances`, `delta`, `threshold`, and `max_n` parameters for full control over matching behavior
- **Deprecates the Java `Coma` class** with a `DeprecationWarning`; `ComaPy` will be renamed to `Coma` in valentine v1.0.0
- Adds 68 tests: regression tests on two datasets (easy + hard with different column names/abbreviations) and unit tests for all internal modules (combination, selection, trigram, TF-IDF, schema graph, datatype similarity)
- Updates README with `ComaPy` documentation and deprecation notice for Java `Coma`

items.title: Fix `ChangeType` `UnsupportedOperationException` on JS `FunctionCall` nodes
items.body:
## Motivation

When running Java recipes (e.g., `NoGuavaPredicate` → `ChangeType`) against repositories containing JavaScript files, `ChangeType.postVisit()` throws `UnsupportedOperationException` on `JS.FunctionCall` nodes. This was discovered in flagship recipe run alerts on app.moderne.io — the "Java best practices" recipe run against Netflix+Spring org produced 46 per-file errors on `metaflow/plugins/cards/ui/prism.js`.

The root cause: `ChangeType.postVisit()` has explicit handling for `J.MethodInvocation` and `J.NewClass` (both `MethodCall` implementations), but `JS.FunctionCall` — also a `MethodCall` — falls through to the generic `TypedTree` branch, which calls `withType()`. All `MethodCall` implementations throw `UnsupportedOperationException` on `withType()` because the type is derived from the method type.

Additionally, `J.ClassDeclaration.type` (a `JavaType.FullyQualified`) was not being serialized over RPC, causing JavaScript class declarations to lose their type attribution when crossing the RPC boundary.

## Summary

- Add a `MethodCall` catch-all branch in `ChangeType.postVisit()` between the `J.NewClass` and `TypedTree` branches, using `withMethodType()` instead of `withType()`
- Add `J.ClassDeclaration.type` to the RPC sender/receiver in all language implementations (Java, TypeScript, C#, Python)
- Add `ChangeTypeAdaptabilityTest` in `rewrite-javascript` that verifies `ChangeType` can handle JS source containing `FunctionCall` nodes

## Test plan

- [x] New `ChangeTypeAdaptabilityTest.functionCallDoesNotThrow()` — applies `ChangeType("Foo", "Bar", false)` to JavaScript containing a class with a method returning a function, verifying both the class rename and that `JS.FunctionCall` nodes don't cause exceptions
- [x] Existing `ChangeTypeTest` in `rewrite-java-test` passes
- [x] Existing `ChangeTypeAdaptabilityTest` in `rewrite-groovy` passes
- [x] Existing `ChangeTypeTest` in `rewrite-kotlin` passes

items.title: Rewrite entrypoint in Python; add bulk_create CSV import
items.body:
## Summary

- Rewrites `entrypoint.sh` from bash to Python 3 (filename preserved — Dockerfile `ENTRYPOINT` and Render `startCommand` unchanged). Still shells out to `psql`/`java` so the existing mock-on-PATH test harness keeps working.
- Adds a `bulk_create` action that downloads a CSV from a signed URL and invokes the JAR's existing `create` path once per row. **No JAR modifications.**
- Introduces `PgClient`, `PgConnInfo`, and `BulkSummary` dataclasses; unifies single-action and bulk row arg-building through one `build_args` helper.
- 53 unit tests + 9 integration tests, all green.

## `bulk_create` contract

- `csv_url` (required, HTTPS) serves a CSV with columns `name, email, company, api_key, notes`
- 10 MB body cap (via `Content-Length` or streaming counter), 60s download timeout, 300s per-row JAR timeout (override via `jar_timeout_secs`)
- Emits a `{total, succeeded, failed, errors}` summary on stdout (and to `result_data` JSONB if `correlation_id` + `result_table` are set)
- Exit `0` on any partial success, `2` when `total > 0 && succeeded == 0` — callers can retry without re-running successful rows
- `csv_url` is never logged on any error path (signed URLs may carry credentials)

## Observability / safety improvements addressed in review

- `download_csv` logs `type(e).__name__: str(e)` (not the URL) so ops can distinguish DNS/TLS/refused/404
- `PgClient` methods return `(ok, stderr)` tuples; `_record_result` surfaces psql's actual error text instead of a generic "failed to write"
- Row errors include `stdout[-500:]` when stderr is empty (JAR OOM with silent stderr is now diagnosable)
- Tempdir rmtree failures logged to stderr (don't silently leak `data-sources.xml` containing DB password)
- `ValidationError` paths now construct the `PgClient` best-effort *before* `validate()` runs so validation failures still land in the result table on first run — integration-tested

## CI compatibility

`docker-compose.test.yml` now sets `extra_hosts: host.docker.internal:host-gateway` so the `bulk_create` integration tests (which serve a CSV from the host to the container) work on Linux CI as well as Docker Desktop.

## Test plan

- [x] `uvx pytest` — 53 unit tests green
- [x] `docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml build && uvx --with psycopg2-binary --with pytest pytest tests/integration/ -v` — 9 integration tests green (includes the bulk_create happy/partial-failure/download-failure paths and the fresh-table validation-error recording case)
- [ ] Smoke test against a Render staging deployment with a real 3-row CSV before rolling to prod

items.title: Python: register all existing recipes
items.body:
## What's changed?

Adding all existing recipes under rewrite-python to the modules `activate()` function.

## What's your motivation?

So that these recipes are installed.
Some recipe libraries already started depending on them and if they are not activated, then the execution might fail with:
```
Caused by io.moderne.jsonrpc.JsonRpcException: {code=-32603, message='Recipe not found: org.openrewrite.python.ChangeImport'}
  io.moderne.jsonrpc.JsonRpc$1.compute(JsonRpc.java:78)
  java.base/java.util.concurrent.RecursiveAction.exec(RecursiveAction.java:194)
```

items.title: Rewriting the Quick Sort algorithm in C#, Java and Python and some minor changes
items.body:
Bartu nasıl olmuş?

Genel olarak C#, Java ve Python kısımları için değişiklikler yaptım. C kısmını etkileyen "değişken adı değiştirme" ve "2 boş satır ekleme" commit'lerim oldu.  Bir de C kısmında iki tane fonksiyonda, değişkenleri her döngüde tekrar tanımlamaması için yapmış olduğum bir commit'im var.

Quick Sort'u yazacaksan benimkinden bakıp yazabilirsin. Hocanın gösterdiği şekilde yazdım. Duplicate elemanlar olursa algoritmanın yavaşlamaması için j'nin durması şeyini de ayarladım kodda. (Yani <= yerine < koydum j için olan while loop'unda.)

Bu arada, moveElementsAndReturnPivot fonksiyonunun içindeki bazı while loop'ları tek bir satır olarak yazmaya çalışma. Daha yavaş oluyor. Denedim.

items.title: feat: add --sanitize option, separate from --content-safety-off
items.body:
## Summary

- Add `--sanitize` boolean CLI option that enables sensitive data masking (emails, URLs, phones, credit cards → placeholders). **Disabled by default.**
- Separate it from `--content-safety-off`, which now exclusively controls rendering-mismatch filters (hidden-text, off-page, tiny, hidden-ocg)
- Deprecate `--content-safety-off sensitive-data` with a stderr warning (still accepted, no-op)

### Why

`--content-safety-off` mixed two fundamentally different concerns:
- **Rendering-mismatch filters** (security): remove invisible content that could be prompt injection
- **Sensitive data masking** (privacy): replace visible, legitimate content with placeholders

These have opposite defaults (security ON, masking OFF) and opposite user intent. Splitting them makes the API clearer.

### Changes

| Area | Files | What |
|------|-------|------|
| Java CLI | `CLIOptions.java` | Add `--sanitize`, deprecate `sensitive-data` value, update `all` case |
| Java Tests | `CLIOptionsContentSafetyTest.java` (new) | 5 tests covering flag, defaults, combinations, deprecation |
| Bindings | `options.json`, generated Python/Node/MDX files | Regenerated via `npm run sync` |
| Node Tests | `convert-options.test.ts` | sanitize in buildArgs + all-options test |
| Python Tests | `test_cli_options.py` | sanitize option existence |
| Docs | `ai-safety.mdx`, `README.md`, Python/Node READMEs | Rewrite filter documentation |

## Test plan

- [x] Java: 406 tests pass (`mvn test`)
- [x] Node: 18 tests pass (`npm test`)
- [x] Python: 38 tests pass (`pytest`)
- [ ] Manual: `opendataloader-pdf test.pdf --sanitize` enables masking
- [ ] Manual: `opendataloader-pdf test.pdf --content-safety-off sensitive-data` prints deprecation warning
- [ ] Manual: `opendataloader-pdf test.pdf --sanitize --content-safety-off all` → sanitize ON, rendering OFF

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

- **17 Railway services provisioned** with sleep-on-idle for all starters
- **Aimock integration** — 37 fixture rules covering all 9 demo types, per-package playwright configs wired, docker-compose for CI
- **CI updated** — 17 starter services in deploy workflow, drift detection CI for template consistency
- **Shell updated** — `npx degit` clone commands, full component tree in Code tab, `.java` file support

## What Changed

### Phase 1A: Starter Template + Generation
- Canonical template at `showcase/starters/template/` with Sales Dashboard, 5 renderer strategies, per-language Dockerfiles (non-root user, agent health checks)
- Generation script at `showcase/scripts/generate-starters.ts` with Python import rewriting, TypeScript shared-tools rewriting, `--check` mode for CI drift detection, deterministic output (sorted devDependencies)
- 537 tests: 267 unit (generation script + transform functions) + 270 cross-starter consistency

### Phase 1B: Demo Revert

- Zero `@copilotkit/showcase-shared` imports in any demo page

### Phase 3: Aimock Integration

- Updated all 17 `showcase/packages/*/playwright.config.ts` with env passthrough for `OPENAI_BASE_URL`/`OPENAI_API_KEY`

items.title: fix(comsol): add run_file so /run path works end-to-end
items.body:
## Summary

- `ComsolDriver.run_file()` was missing, so `sim.runner.execute_script(driver=...)` crashed with `AttributeError` for every COMSOL script. This regressed (latently) in the unified DriverProtocol refactor (f428ab6), which routed COMSOL through the one-shot `/run` path but never added the execution method.
- Add `run_file()` matching the PyFluent driver's pattern (shell out to `sys.executable`).
- Replace a placebo `TestRunFile` test with two real ones — the old test monkeypatched `execute_script` then called `execute_script` directly, so it never touched the driver.

## Root cause

`server.py /run` → `runner.execute_script(driver=driver)` → `driver.run_file(script)` (see `src/sim/runner.py:47`). Before this PR, `ComsolDriver` had no `run_file`, so every call raised `AttributeError: 'ComsolDriver' object has no attribute 'run_file'`.

## Changes

- `src/sim/drivers/comsol/driver.py` — add `run_file(self, script)` that delegates to `run_subprocess([sys.executable, str(script)], ...)`. Keeps the driver SDK-free at module load; the subprocess inherits whichever `mph`/JPype/Java is present in the env.
- `tests/test_comsol_driver.py` — rewrite `TestRunFile`:
  - `test_run_file_invokes_python_subprocess`: asserts the subprocess command starts with `[sys.executable, script]`.
  - `test_run_file_routes_through_execute_script`: asserts `runner.execute_script(driver=driver)` completes without `AttributeError` — the exact integration path that was broken.

## Test plan

- [x] Offline suite: 84 passed on macOS (was 83; +1 new real test).
- [x] Live on Windows (win1) with COMSOL 6.4.0.293 + `mph` 1.3.1 + JPype 1.7.0: `driver.run_file(script)` → `mph.start()` boots the Java client → `exit_code=0`, stdout `{"ok": true, "java_version": "6.4", "models": []}`.
- [ ] Reviewer: sanity-check the subprocess pattern matches PyFluent (`src/sim/drivers/fluent/driver.py:264`).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

items.title: feat: Python/Polars GTFS Validator Migration (SpecOps Rewrite)
items.body:
# Python/Polars GTFS Validator Migration

## Overview

This PR introduces a complete rewrite of the GTFS validator from Java to modern Python using Polars for tabular data processing. This follows the **SpecOps methodology** for systematic migration and validation.

## SpecOps Migration Approach

This rewrite follows the SpecOps methodology which emphasizes:

1. **Behavioral Equivalence** - The Python validator produces output equivalent to the Java validator (`report.json`, `system_errors.json`)
2. **Clean Architecture** - Orchestrator owns I/O, parsing, schema enforcement; validators own pure domain logic
3. **Incremental Validation** - Each validator is tested against real feeds with diff analysis
4. **No Framework Parity** - We don't port Java framework patterns (reflection, DI containers, code generation)

## Key Changes

### Core Architecture
- `src/gtfs_validator/` - Python package with clean module separation
- `runner.py` - Orchestration pipeline
- `schemas.py` - Schema registry for GTFS tables
- `loading.py` - Polars-based data loading
- `validators/` - Cross-table business validations

### Validators Migrated
See `specops/validator-list.md` for complete migration status.

### Documentation
- `specops/llm-validator-authoring.md` - Template for LLM-assisted validator authoring
- `specops/validator-migration-prompt.md` - Migration prompt template
- Updated `AGENTS.md` with Python development guidelines

## Stack

- Python 3.12+
- Polars for tabular data
- `uv` for dependency management
- `pytest` for testing
- `mypy`/`pyright` for type checking
- `ruff` for linting/formatting

## Testing

```bash
uv sync
uv run pytest -q
uv run ruff check .
uv run mypy src
```

## Notes

This is a **rewrite**, not a line-by-line port. The Python implementation:
- Uses idiomatic Python patterns
- Leverages Polars for vectorized operations
- Maintains strict separation of concerns
- Preserves behavioral equivalence with Java output

---

**Review focus**: Architecture correctness, validator behavior parity, test coverage.

items.title: Rewrite calendar app in Java with Spring Boot
items.body:
## Summary
- Rewrites the Python Flask calendar app as a Java 17 Spring Boot application
- Uses Thymeleaf with layout dialect for server-side HTML rendering
- All three views (month, week, day) preserved with identical CSS styling and navigation
- Maven build with Spring Boot 3.4.3

## Project structure
| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `pom.xml` | Maven build config with Spring Boot + Thymeleaf dependencies |
| `CalendarApplication.java` | Spring Boot entry point |
| `CalendarController.java` | Routes: `/`, `/month/{y}/{m}`, `/week/{y}/{w}`, `/day/{y}/{m}/{d}` |
| `CalendarUtils.java` | Date logic — month grids, ISO week calculations, formatting |
| `DayInfo.java` / `HourInfo.java` / `NavContext.java` | Record types for template data |
| `src/main/resources/templates/` | Thymeleaf templates (layout, month, week, day) |
| `src/main/resources/static/styles.css` | Identical CSS from previous implementation |

## Test plan
- [ ] Build with `mvn clean package`
- [ ] Run with `mvn spring-boot:run` and verify at `http://localhost:3000`
- [ ] Verify month view renders with correct grid and today highlighting
- [ ] Verify week view renders with time slots and current hour indicator
- [ ] Verify day view renders with hourly schedule
- [ ] Verify view switching and Today button navigation

items.title: feat: language-agnostic Helm chart and arbitrary namespace support
items.body:
## Summary
- **Language-agnostic agent chart (#580)**: Remove Python bias from `mcp-mesh-agent` Helm chart. Add `agent.runtime` field and `isPython` helper for conditional Python env var injection. Remove dead `agent.script` and `agent.python` fields. Rewrite README with Python/TypeScript/Java examples.
- **Arbitrary namespace support (#579)**: Replace hardcoded FQDN hostnames with short names in `mcp-mesh-core` values. Add `| default .Release.Namespace` fallback to namespace template. Add `networkPolicy.allowedNamespace` for dynamic ingress rules. Document custom namespace, multi-tenant, and cross-namespace patterns.
- **ENTRYPOINT/CMD alignment**: Fix TypeScript runtime ENTRYPOINT (`node` → `npx tsx`). Align all scaffold Dockerfiles so CMD only provides the script/jar path (ENTRYPOINT provides the runtime command). Update Go handlers to match.
- **NetworkPolicy fix**: AND namespaceSelector with podSelector in registry ingress (was incorrectly OR'd as separate `from` entries).
- **Scaffold improvements**: Add `agent.runtime` to all helm-values templates. Add Docker/K8s/Documentation sections to Java/Python basic READMEs. Remove obsolete "Adding Tools" sections. Add cross-namespace comments to registryUrl.

## Review Notes
- Short service names replace FQDNs in mcp-mesh-core defaults — intentional for namespace flexibility, but a breaking change for cross-namespace users (documented override path)
- Duplicate PYTHONUNBUFFERED in ConfigMap + deployment env (minor, explicit env wins)

Closes #579
Closes #580

## Test plan
- [x] `helm lint` passes for all 3 charts (agent, core, registry)
- [x] `helm template` Python agent includes PYTHONUNBUFFERED/PYTHONPATH/UVICORN_* env vars
- [x] `helm template` TypeScript/Java agent does NOT include Python env vars
- [x] `helm template` with `--set agent.runtime=python` injects Python env vars regardless of image name
- [x] Registry NetworkPolicy renders AND'd selectors (namespace + podSelector in same from entry)
- [x] `go build` and `go test` pass for handlers
- [ ] Deploy Python/TypeScript/Java agents to K8s cluster and verify registration

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Multi-runtime agent support with runtime detection (Python, TypeScript, Java).
  * New agent.runtime configuration field.

* **Improvements**
  * Container images and starter commands updated to rely on base image entrypoints.
  * Network policies made namespace-aware with explicit ingress ports and allowedNamespace.
  * Service hostnames default to in‑namespace short names.

* **Documentation**
  * Expanded docs and Helm notes for custom namespaces, cross‑namespace access, and multi‑runtime deployments.

items.title: NO-SNOW: Improve ConfigManager implementation
items.body:
## Summary

- Add `bool_value` support to `ConfigSetting` protobuf message
- Add `ConfigGetPaths` RPC so Python can resolve config file locations from sf_core
- Pass explicit file paths (`config_file`, `connections_file`) in `ConfigLoadAllSectionsRequest` so Python controls which files are read
- Rewrite Python `ConfigManager` to delegate **all** file I/O, TOML parsing, and permission checks to sf_core (removes `tomlkit`/`platformdirs` dependency from Python side)
- Add error translation from Rust core `DriverException` to Python `ConfigManagerError`/`ConfigSourceError`
- Fix `MissingConfigOptionError` hierarchy (now extends `ConfigSourceError`)
- Add `constants.py` re-exporting `CONFIG_FILE` and `CONNECTIONS_FILE`
- Handle root-level TOML keys in `config_manager.rs`
- Fix `toml_loader` to handle `try_exists()` errors gracefully
- Add `test_config_manager_old_tests.py` for backward compatibility coverage

Split from turbaszek-backcompat-3 — ConfigManager-only changes.

## Test plan
- [ ] Unit tests pass: `python/tests/unit/test_config_manager.py`
- [ ] Integration tests pass: `python/tests/integ/config/`
- [ ] Rust config_manager tests pass
- [ ] Verify config file path resolution works across platforms


Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

items.title: Rewrite tooling in Java and drop Python scripts
items.body:
### Motivation
- Replace the existing Python-based tooling with Java implementations so the project is maintained in Java as requested (`refais tout en java / supprime le python`).
- Provide CLI programs that work with standard Java tooling and remove the Python dependency footprint.

### Description
- Added two Java CLI programs: `com.ratemywine.CollectRatings` (CSV-driven DuckDuckGo search + regex extraction) and `com.ratemywine.MilleniumWineRatingsScraper` (site crawler extracting JSON-LD and text ratings) under `src/main/java/com/ratemywine/`.
- Updated `README.md` with Java 17+ compilation (`javac -d out src/main/java/com/ratemywine/*.java`) and usage instructions for both tools.
- Removed the legacy Python scripts `millenium_wine_ratings_scraper.py` and `scripts/collect_ratings.py` and updated `requirements.txt` to indicate Python is no longer used.
- Added simple CLI parsing helpers and CSV/JSON output routines in the Java implementations to match previous output formats.

### Testing
- Compiled the Java sources with `javac -d out src/main/java/com/ratemywine/*.java` which succeeded.
- Ran the crawler with `java -cp out com.ratemywine.MilleniumWineRatingsScraper https://example.com --max-pages 1 --min-delay 0 --max-delay 0 --timeout 5 --csv /tmp/wine.csv --json /tmp/wine.json` and the program executed but failed HTTP requests due to environment network restrictions (`Network is unreachable`).
- Ran the CSV-driven flow with a sample file via `java -cp out com.ratemywine.CollectRatings --input /tmp/wines.csv --output /tmp/ratings.csv --max-results 1 --timeout 3 --sleep 0` which produced a valid output CSV containing a `no-candidate-page` status (expected given no network search results).

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_69a0bccd203883219fb6778344d7a9b1)

items.title: feat: TPS architecture rewrite — extensible app platform with stateless OAuth
items.body:
## Summary

Complete rewrite of the Python TPS from a single-app GitHub OAuth service into an extensible app integration platform, modeled after the Java TPS at Atomicwork.

### Architecture
- **TPS as SDK** — Core imports TPS functions directly (no HTTP), own DB connection to `tps` database
- **Stateless OAuth** — Core generates encrypted state (Fernet), TPS just builds URLs. Core's public `/oauth/callback` decrypts state and exchanges code.
- **OpenAPI-first** — specs in `openapi/*.yaml`, Pydantic DTOs generated via `make generate-api`
- **Alembic migrations** — static versioned migrations replacing `create_all` + seed functions

### Key changes
- 5 auth types supported: OAuth2, API Key, Basic Auth, Form-based OAuth2, mTLS
- Int-backed enums for auth_type/category/provider (stored as SMALLINT, resolved to labels in API)
- App marketplace with meta JSON (icon, description, form fields for dynamic frontend rendering)
- Per-app handler pattern: OAuthHandler + CredentialHandler protocols
- MultiFernet encryption with key rotation support
- Token refresh with SELECT FOR UPDATE (race condition protection)
- User-scoped integrations (replaced workspace_id with user_id)
- Separate Core (esd:esd@11432/core) and TPS (tps:tps@11432/tps) databases
- Migration naming convention: `V<app_code>_<epoch>__<migration_type>.py`

### E2E tested
- Full GitHub OAuth flow verified: install → GitHub authorize → callback → token exchange → integration saved
- `NandishNaik01` connected successfully via the new stateless flow

### Endpoints

**Apps:**
| Method | Path | Purpose |
|--------|------|---------|
| GET | /apps | List active apps |
| GET | /apps/{identifier} | Get by name or code |

**Integrations:**
| Method | Path | Purpose |
|--------|------|---------|
| GET | /integrations | List user's integrations |
| GET | /integrations/{identifier} | Get by app_name or ID |
| POST | /integrations/{app}/install | Start OAuth flow |
| POST | /integrations/{app}/connect | Store credentials |
| DELETE | /integrations/{id} | Disconnect |
| GET | /oauth/callback | Public OAuth callback |

## Test plan
- [x] 152 automated tests pass
- [x] E2E: GitHub OAuth flow works end-to-end
- [x] List apps returns GitHub with correct metadata
- [x] Integration saved with identifier "NandishNaik01"

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

items.title: Add Python (GraalPy) transformation support with local file resolution
items.body:
## Overview Adds Python transformation support using [GraalPy](https://www.graalvm.org/python/) (Python 3.12 on GraalVM), enabling users to write document and metadata transformations in Python with full pip package support. Scripts and venvs are resolved from local file paths. ## Architecture ```mermaid graph TD subgraph "Transformer Interface" ITP["IJsonTransformerProvider
(SPI)"] end subgraph "Python Transformer" JPP["JsonPythonTransformerProvider"] PT["PythonTransformer
(GraalPy 25.0.2 / Python 3.12)"] end subgraph "User Artifacts" LF["Local files / dirs"] end JPP -->|implements| ITP JPP -->|reads| LF JPP -->|creates| PT ``` ## New Modules | Module | Purpose | |--------|---------| | `jsonPythonTransformer` | Core `PythonTransformer` engine — evaluates Python via GraalVM Polyglot API | | `jsonPythonTransformerProvider` | SPI provider — config parsing, script resolution, tarball extraction | | `custom_transform/` | Complete standalone Python project example with pydantic, pytest, deployment docs | ## `JsonPythonTransformerProvider` Config | Key | Required | Description | |-----|----------|-------------| | `initializationScriptFile` | One of three | Local file path to a `.py` file | | `initializationScript` | One of three | Inline Python source code | | `initializationResourcePath` | One of three | Classpath resource path | | `bindingsObject` | Yes | JSON string passed to `main(context)` | | `pythonModulePath` | No | Local directory or `.tar.gz` file containing a GraalPy venv | ## User Workflow ```bash # 1. Build locally with standard Python tooling cd custom_transform && pipenv install --dev && pipenv run pytest # 2. Create GraalPy venv with your packages graalpy -m venv /tmp/venv && /tmp/venv/bin/pip install ./custom_transform # 3. Reference in transformer config (local paths) --doc-transformer-config '[{ "JsonPythonTransformerProvider": { "initializationScriptFile": "/path/to/entry_point.py", "bindingsObject": "{}", "pythonModulePath": "/path/to/venv" } }]' ``` ## Testing - `PythonTransformerTest`: 4 tests — core engine, logging, map modification - `JsonPythonTransformerProviderTest`: 14 tests — config validation, script sources, tarball extraction, error paths - `PythonTransformationE2ETest`: 2 tests — full SPI pipeline - `PythonDocTransformE2ETest`: 1 test — document index rewrite + field addition - `PythonMetadataTransformE2ETest`: 1 test — field type rewriting - All existing transformer tests pass (no regressions) ## Bundled Example Scripts Two Python scripts are bundled as classpath resources for common use cases: - `python/doc_transform.py` — index name rewriting and field injection for RFS backfill - `python/metadata_transform.py` — field type rewriting for metadata migration (e.g. `string` → `text`) ## Image Size Impact Measured via `runtimeClasspath` resolution (the jars that go into Docker images): | Component | main | PR branch | Delta | |-----------|------|-----------|-------| | **Traffic Replayer** | 110.7 MB (144 jars) | 240.8 MB (163 jars) | **+130.1 MB** (+19 jars) | | **RFS (DocumentsFromSnapshotMigration)** | 150.6 MB (131 jars) | 278.9 MB (150 jars) | **+128.3 MB** (+19 jars) | The replayer already shares `icu4j` (18 MB), `truffle-api` (14 MB), `polyglot` (504 KB), and `truffle-runtime` (841 KB) with the JS transformer — these are not new. Breakdown of new jars: | Jar | Size | Notes | |-----|------|-------| | `python-language-25.0.2.jar` | 98 MB | GraalPy interpreter (Python 3.12) | | `python-resources-25.0.2.jar` | 15 MB | Python stdlib | | `truffle-nfi-*.jar` (3 jars) | 3.9 MB | Native function interface | | `commons-compress-1.26.2.jar` | 1.1 MB | Tarball extraction | | `profiler-tool-25.0.2.jar` | 507 KB | GraalVM profiler (transitive) | | `commons-io`, `python-embedding`, other | ~1 MB | Small deps | | `jsonPythonTransformer*.jar` | 19 KB | Our code | The 98 MB `python-language` jar is the dominant cost — comparable to the existing `js-community` jar (GraalJS) already in the replayer.

items.title: docs: Reorganize documentation structure
items.body:
## Summary

Reorganizes documentation structure to improve discoverability and separate development concepts from operational concerns.

## Changes

### New Operations Guide
- Created `operations-guide-index.md` as landing page
- Moved configuration, authentication, security, LLM/embedding providers, vector store backends from Developer Guide

### SDK Documentation
- **Python SDK**: Complete rewrite with accurate API examples, `MemoryClientConfig` usage, tool integration, filter reference
- **TypeScript SDK**: New documentation (358 lines)
- **Java SDK**: New documentation (321 lines)
- All SDKs now under API Reference → Client SDKs

### Cleanup
- Consolidated AWS Bedrock content into `llm-providers.md`, deleted duplicate `aws-bedrock.md`
- Fixed broken anchor link in `langchain-integration.md`
- Updated `mkdocs.yml` navigation

### Navigation Structure
```
Developer Guide: Memory concepts, integration patterns, LangChain
Operations Guide: Configuration, Auth, Security, Providers
API Reference: REST, MCP, CLI, Client SDKs (Python, TypeScript, Java)
```

## Testing
- `mkdocs build --strict` passes
- All grammarian style checks pass (Excellent ratings)

## Notes
- `python-sdk-index.md` is now orphaned (not in nav) - can be deleted in follow-up if desired

items.title: chore: remove legacy microservices, infra files, and rewrite .gitignore for Python
items.body: # chore: remove legacy microservices, infra, and rewrite .gitignore ## Summary Bulk deletion of all legacy microservice directories, infrastructure/build tooling, and other files that are no longer part of the monolith-based repo structure. Replaces the 470-line Java/.NET/Node `.gitignore` with a focused Python/React `.gitignore`. **Deleted service directories:** `account-service/`, `position-service/`, `trade-service/`, `trade-processor/`, `people-service/`, `reference-data/`, `trade-feed/`, `database/`, `ingress/` **Deleted infra & build tooling:** `docker-compose.yml`, `.devcontainer/`, `gitops/`, `.github/workflows/`, `gradle/`, `gradlew`, `gradlew.bat`, `settings.gradle`, `netlify.toml`, `renovate.json`, `.dockerignore`, `radius-traderx/`, `.nvmrc`, `.sdkmanrc` **Deleted other:** `web-front-end/angular/`, `web-front-end/.gitignore`, `web-front-end/README.md`, `website/` (Docusaurus), `LOCAL_STARTUP.md` **Additionally deleted** (not in original spec but clearly legacy): `.github/dotnet-cve-ignore-list.xml`, `.github/gradle-cve-ignore-list.xml`, `.github/node-cve-ignore-list.xml` **Preserved:** `traderx-monolith/`, `web-front-end/react/`, `docs/`, `LICENSE`, `LICENSE.spdx`, `NOTICE`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `README.md`, `.github/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`, `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/` ## Review & Testing Checklist for Human - [ ] **Verify preserved files are intact**: Confirm `traderx-monolith/`, `web-front-end/react/`, and `docs/` directories still exist with all expected contents on the branch. The diff is deletion-only so these should be untouched, but worth a spot-check. - [ ] **Approve CVE ignore list deletions**: Three `.github/*-cve-ignore-list.xml` files were deleted beyond the original spec. These referenced Java/H2/OkHttp/.NET/Node CVEs for the now-deleted services. Confirm this is acceptable or if they should be restored. - [ ] **Validate `.gitignore` content**: The new `.gitignore` covers Python/SQLite/Node/IDE/OS patterns. Review if any additional patterns are needed for the monolith project. ### Notes - This PR branches from `devin/1772745102-traderx-monolith` (not `main`) to preserve the monolith code added in PR #2. - No legacy infrastructure artifacts (Dockerfile, *.gradle, docker-compose*, *.bicep) remain in the repo after this cleanup. - No code changes or functional changes - purely deletions and `.gitignore` rewrite. --- **Session**: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/3dad6922763c4eb292f032f9b7614c66 **Requested by**: @mbatchelor81 ---

items.title: [ZEPPELIN-6404] Rewrite merge PR script in Python with no external dependencies
items.body:
## Summary

- Rewrite `dev/merge_zeppelin_pr.py` as a single-file Python CLI (`dev/merge_pr.py`)
- No external dependencies — uses only Python 3 built-in libraries (`urllib`, `json`, `subprocess`, `argparse`, `re`)
- Runs directly with `python3 dev/merge_pr.py --pr <number> [flags]`
- Non-interactive, AI-agent friendly design

## Motivation

The existing Python merge script (`dev/merge_zeppelin_pr.py`) requires a Python virtual environment with dependencies (`jira`, `python-dotenv`), and is interactive (prompts for user input). This makes it difficult to use from CI/CD or AI agent workflows.

The new version:
- Requires only Python 3 (no venv, no pip install)
- All flags are CLI arguments — no interactive prompts
- Single file, ~450 lines, zero external dependencies
- Instance-based design (`MergePR` class)

## Usage

```bash
# Dry run (shows PR info without merging)
python3 dev/merge_pr.py --pr 5167 --dry-run

# Merge and resolve JIRA
python3 dev/merge_pr.py --pr 5167 --resolve-jira --fix-versions 0.13.0

# Merge and cherry-pick into release branches
python3 dev/merge_pr.py --pr 5167 --release-branches branch-0.12,branch-0.11
```

## Flags

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--pr` | Pull request number (required) |
| `--target` | Target branch (default: PR base branch) |
| `--fix-versions` | JIRA fix version(s), comma-separated |
| `--release-branches` | Release branch(es) to cherry-pick into, comma-separated |
| `--resolve-jira` | Resolve associated JIRA issue(s) |
| `--dry-run` | Show what would be done without making changes |
| `--push-remote` | Git remote for pushing (default: `apache`) |

Tokens are read from environment variables: `GITHUB_OAUTH_KEY`, `JIRA_ACCESS_TOKEN`.

## Test Plan

- [x] `python3 dev/merge_pr.py --help` — shows usage
- [x] `python3 dev/merge_pr.py --pr 5167 --dry-run` — fetches PR info correctly
- [ ] Full merge test with a real PR
- [ ] Cherry-pick into release branch test
- [ ] JIRA resolution test

items.title: Python: Add RPC infrastructure for calling Java recipes
items.body:
## Summary
- Add `JavaRewriteRpc` standalone RPC server in rewrite-maven that Python spawns as subprocess
- Add Python RPC client (`JavaRpcClient`) to manage the Java subprocess lifecycle
- Add Python recipe wrappers that delegate to Java: `ChangeType`, `ChangeMethodName`, `ChangePackage`, `DeleteMethodArgument`, `ReorderMethodArguments`, `AddLiteralMethodArgument`
- Add precondition helpers: `uses_type`, `uses_method`, `find_types`, `find_methods`
- Add `Markup` markers: `MarkupWarn`, `MarkupError`, `MarkupInfo`, `MarkupDebug`
- Add additional `MarkerPrinter` variants: `VERBOSE`, `FENCED`, `SANITIZED`

items.title: ● docs: align 2026 release notes with actual CHANGES history
items.body:
- Move Unit 1.35.0 (Sep 3, 2025, upstream F5/NGINX postmortem release) from news/2026 to news/2025 with accurate changelog content
  - Rewrite Unit 1.35.1 (Apr 3, 2026): first FreeUnit release — GHCR Docker images, trixie base, PHP 8.4/8.5 + Python 3.14 CI, OpenSSL 3.6 CI, community fork rebrand
  - Rewrite Unit 1.35.2 (Apr 4, 2026): PHP TrueAsync graceful shutdown fix, NXT_PHP_PRE_REQUEST_INIT guard, Ruby clang-ast CI fix
  - Update Unit 1.35.3 (Apr 7, 2026): add packages.freeunit.org mirror migration and unitctl GPG key URL change
  - Update 2026/index.rst: remove non-existent FreeUnit 1.35.0 Apr entry, fix dates and descriptions for 1.35.1 and 1.35.2

  - Create source/news/2026/ with index.rst covering four releases
  - 1.35.0: initial FreeUnit fork — PHP 8.4/8.5, Ruby 3.4, Docker trixie, OpenTelemetry in CI
  - 1.35.1: fix PHP 8.5 worker hang on shutdown (nxt_php_quit_handler, ZEND_ASYNC_SHUTDOWN)
  - 1.35.2: upgrade to OpenSSL 3.6, replace deprecated EVP/SSLeay APIs, eclipse-temurin jammy → noble
  - 1.35.3: ARM64 CI speedup, njs 0.9.6, libunit-wasm 0.5.0, wasmtime 35→43, Rust 1.89→1.94, Java 11 → 17/21
  - Add 2026 to news.rst toctree and archive list

items.title: Rewrite catalog from decompiled Bose Music APK
items.body:
## Summary

- Rewrites the device catalog (Python / Rust / C++) using `io.intrepid.bose_bmap.model.enums.BoseProductId` from the decompiled Bose Music APK as the authoritative source.
- Expands from 14 → 39 entries and corrects several misattributed PIDs that were inherited from `bose-dfu` / `downloads.bose.com/lookup.xml` (those use different ID spaces — USB DFU PIDs and firmware lookup codes aren't BT Modalias PIDs).
- Adds `0x4062 edith "QuietComfort Ultra Earbuds (2nd Gen)"` with `qc_ultra2` config, verified by the reporter in #13.

## Why it matters

Prior catalog mappings were wrong for most entries — only WOLVERINE (`0x4082`) and BAYWOLF (`0x4020`) matched the APK. Notable corrections:

| PID | Was | Now (APK) |
|---|---|---|
| `0x4017` | kleos / QuietComfort 35 | kleos / SoundWear (neck speaker) |
| `0x4060` | olivia / QC Earbuds II | olivia / Frames Tempo |
| `0x4061` | vedder / QC 45 | vedder / Frames |
| `0x4063` | edith / Ultra Open Earbuds | (removed — it's EDITH_CHARGING_CASE) |
| `0x4075` | prince / QC Ultra Earbuds | prince / QuietComfort Headphones |
| `0x4039` | duran / SoundLink Flex | duran / QuietComfort 45 |
| `0x403A` | gwen / SoundLink Revolve+ II | gwen / Sport Open Earbuds |
| `0x402F` | lando / Portable Home Speaker | lando / QC Earbuds |
| `0x402D` | revel / Home Speaker 300 | revel / Sport Earbuds |
| `0x4066` | lonestarr / SoundLink Max | lonestarr / QC Ultra Headphones |
| `0x4073` | scotty / SoundLink Flex 2nd Gen | (removed — it's SCOTTY_CHARGING_CASE; real scotty earbuds is `0x4072`) |

New additions include WOLFCASTLE (original QC35), SCOTTY (original QC Ultra Earbuds), SERENA (Ultra Open Earbuds), EDITH (QC Ultra 2 Earbuds), plus the full Frames / SoundSport / SoundLink lineups for recognition-only detection.

## Config support

Only entries with a corroborated protocol path carry a `config` key:

| PID | Codename | Product | Config |
|---|---|---|---|
| `0x400C` | wolfcastle | QuietComfort 35 | `qc35` (new, same family as baywolf) |
| `0x4020` | baywolf | QuietComfort 35 II | `qc35` (unchanged, verified) |
| `0x4062` | edith | QC Ultra Earbuds (2nd Gen) | `qc_ultra2` (new, verified by #13) |
| `0x4082` | wolverine | QC Ultra Headphones (2nd Gen) | `qc_ultra2` (unchanged, verified) |

Closes #13 — the reporter's QC Ultra Earbuds (Gen 2) will now auto-discover with the `qc_ultra2` config, no manual `--device` flag needed.

## Test plan

- [x] Python: `pytest` — 120 passed, 18 skipped
- [x] Rust: `cargo test` — 60 passed
- [x] C++: `bmap_tests` — 52 passed
- [x] Docs updated (`README.md`, `docs/architecture.md`) to reflect new supported list and call out the DFU/Modalias PID-space distinction
- [ ] @stevenart to confirm auto-detection on merged build

items.title: Documentation from telemetry on the server
items.body:
## Description
Gathered server telemetry via terminal commands to understand the current state of the server's performance and identify potential issues.

## Related Issue
Closes #206 

## Type of Change
- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Rewrite (Python → Ruby)
- [x] Documentation
- [ ] DevOps / Infrastructure
- [x] Chore

## Changes Made
- Ran `top`, `uptime`, `free -m`, `df -h`, `iftop` and `nethogs` on the server
- Documented results and anomalies in `docs/choices-and-challenges/`
- Flagged two anomalies to the team: no swap configured and memory usage at 57%

## How to Test
1. SSH into the server
2. Run the commands listed in the documentation
3. Verify results match the documented baseline

## Checklist
- [x] Tested locally
- [ ] OpenAPI spec updated (if endpoint changed)
- [x] No hardcoded secrets or credentials
- [ ] Database migrations work (if applicable)

* **Documentation**
  * Added a new server telemetry section with a step-by-step terminal procedure for assessing server health.
  * Documents observed states (no swap configured, high memory usage with little free RAM, restricted kernel message access), a remediation checklist (create/enable swap, persist changes, relax dmesg restriction), listed diagnostic commands for CPU/memory/disk/network/oom investigation, and placeholder retrospective/learning notes.

items.title: (docs): Remove all orphan pages, promote debugging, add agent skills guide

labels.name: documentation
labels.description: Improvements or additions to documentation

labels.name: enhancement
labels.description: New feature or request

items.body:
Audited `docs/` for pages that exist on disk but aren't in navigation. Found 19 orphans across five groups; this PR cleans all of them up.

## What changed

**Top-level orphans** — `faqs.mdx`, `use-local-mcp-server.mdx`, `building-a-client-node.mdx`, `building-mcp-with-llms.mdx`. The first three duplicated content that's already in the intro and build guides, so they're deleted with redirects. The fourth was a dated "paste llms-full.txt into chat" tutorial — replaced with a new [Build with Agent Skills](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/docs/develop/build-with-agent-skills) page covering the [`mcp-server-dev` plugin](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-plugins-official/tree/main/plugins/mcp-server-dev) and added to nav above build-server.

**Spec security_best_practices files** — the 2025-06-18 and 2025-11-25 copies already had redirects pointing at the tutorials version. The source files were dead weight; deleted. Verified all 13 inbound anchor links (`#token-passthrough`, `#confused-deputy-problem`, etc.) resolve on the redirect target.

**Debugging guide** — promoted from `legacy/tools/` to `docs/tools/` with a rewrite. Fixed the SDK logging examples (wrong Python API, missing `await`), dropped the "macOS only" caveat and added Windows tabs, added a Streamable HTTP note, corrected the DevTools shortcut, and cross-linked into the spec for lifecycle/transport/logging anchors. The Claude Desktop section is now framed as one example rather than the whole page.

**Legacy concepts** — all eight `legacy/concepts/*` pages deleted with redirects to `/specification/latest/*`. Before deleting, diffed each against its draft spec counterpart. Six had nothing worth keeping. Two yielded salvage:

- `resources.mdx` draft spec gained: multiple-contents-per-read note, `resources/unsubscribe` example + mermaid arrows, directory traversal MUST
- `tools.mdx` draft spec gained: note on cross-server tool name collisions

**Java SDK stubs** — three 12-line "documentation has moved" pages pointing at `java.sdk.modelcontextprotocol.io`. Deleted; the inline links in the Java tabs of build-server and build-client now go directly to the SDK site.

The `docs/legacy/`, `docs/sdk/`, and `docs/tutorials/` directories are gone entirely.

## Test plan

- [x] `npm run prep` clean
- [x] No broken links
- [x] No `/legacy/` references left in navigable docs
- [x] Spec anchor cross-links verified against 2025-11-25 headings
- [x] Mintlify preview

items.title: Container-first validation infrastructure
items.body:
## Summary
- Rewrite `validate-local-{common,python,go,java,rust}` to delegate to repo `scripts/dev/*.sh` (which run inside containers via `docker-test`)
- Add `DOCKER_EXTRA_VOLUMES` and language detection fallback to `docker-test`
- Create `validate-local-common-container` for containerised common checks
- Add Docker pre-flight check to `validate_local.py`
- Remove fallback `docker run` blocks from dev scripts; add `uv sync --check` to `audit.sh`
- Net -218 lines: simpler, consistent, container-only validation

## Test plan
- [ ] Run `st-validate-local` in standard-tooling — all checks pass
- [ ] Verify `docker` and `docker-test` missing gives clear error
- [ ] Run `st-validate-local` in a language-less repo with `DOCKER_DEV_IMAGE` set
- [ ] Run full test suite — 249 tests pass at 100% coverage

Closes #204

items.title: Setup repo structure, .gitignore, and professional README
items.body:
Bootstraps the repository to meet required structural and workflow conventions: root directories, a comprehensive `.gitignore`, and a professional English `README.md`.

## Directory structure
- Added `/phase1`, `/phase2`, `/phase3`, `/src`, `/scripts`, `/docs` as required root directories
- Each directory includes a `README.md` describing its scope and contents

## `.gitignore`
Covers all common exclusion categories:
- Runtime artifacts: `node_modules/`, `dist/`, `build/`, `.cache/`
- Secrets & config: `.env`, `*.env`, `*.pem`, `*.key`, `secrets/`
- User data: `uploads/`, `logs/`, `*.log`
- IaC state: `.terraform/`, `*.tfstate`, `*.tfvars`
- OS/IDE noise: `.DS_Store`, `.vscode/`, `.idea/`
- Language build outputs: Python `__pycache__`, Java `target/`, etc.

## README.md
Rewrites the placeholder `# DEVOPS` with a full professional document:
- Repo structure table mapping each directory to its purpose
- **Branch strategy** section: feature-branch workflow, no direct commits to `main`, all changes via PR with ≥1 reviewer approval and clear description
- Contributing guide using [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) format
- Team table and getting-started instructions

> **Note:** Branch protection rules on `main` (require PR, require review) must be enabled by a repo admin in **Settings → Branches → Branch protection rules** — this cannot be enforced via committed files.

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

📱 Kick off Copilot coding agent tasks wherever you are with [GitHub Mobile](https://gh.io/cca-mobile-docs), available on iOS and Android.

items.title: divin-fix: remediate CAST issues in WebgoatProperties.java (4 issues)
items.body: # divin-fix: remediate CAST issues in WebgoatProperties.java ## Summary Automated CAST Imaging Express remediation for `WebgoatProperties.java`. Two categories of changes: 1. **`e.printStackTrace()` → `System.err.println("Error: " + e.getMessage())`** — 1 location in `main()` catch block. Addresses CAST `printStackTrace` anti-pattern (stack trace leakage). 2. **`// CAST: Consider using a proper logging framework`** — 3 comment annotations above `System.out.println` calls (constructor, `main()` × 2). **No behavioral change** for these. ⚠️ The diff appears as a full-file rewrite (~123 lines removed, ~126 added) because the automated regex script rewrote the file. Only the 4 edits above are intentional. ## Review & Testing Checklist for Human - [ ] **Verify no accidental content/logic changes from the full-file rewrite**: GitHub's diff shows a complete rewrite. Run `diff -bw` between this branch and `WebGoat_5.2_Devin` to confirm only the 4 intended edits exist. In particular, verify `getIntProperty()`, `getBooleanProperty()`, `trimLesson()`, and the `FileInputStream` constructor logic are byte-identical to the base. - [ ] **Evaluate `getMessage()` returning null in `main()`**: The original `e.printStackTrace()` provided a full stack trace. The replacement `System.err.println("Error: " + e.getMessage())` loses all stack context, and `getMessage()` can return `null` for some `IOException` subtypes, yielding `"Error: null"`. Decide if this diagnostic regression is acceptable for the `main()` test harness. - [ ] **Compile the file** (`javac -Xlint`) — no compilation was performed before this commit. Confirm no encoding corruption was introduced by the regex rewrite. ### Notes - Applied via automated Python regex script — no manual review or compilation was performed before commit. - The 3 `System.out.println` calls were annotated with comments but **not replaced** — this PR makes zero behavioral change for those lines. - Pre-existing bug: `main()` can NPE on `properties.getProperty(...)` if the constructor throws — not introduced by this PR. - No logging framework exists in this codebase; `System.err.println` is the best available substitute without adding a dependency. Link to Devin run: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/d40f9f0f9c5e43ddb8a405cd743e5f0b Requested by: @theabhisheksinha ---

items.title: feat: v1.0.0 — Rust core rewrite with polyglot bindings
items.body:
## Summary

Complete rewrite of tree-sitter-language-pack from Python to Rust with polyglot language bindings supporting 168+ grammars.

- **Rust core** (`ts-pack-core`): Thread-safe `LanguageRegistry` with dynamic parser loading, feature-gated language selection
- **C-FFI** (`ts-pack-ffi`): cbindgen-generated headers, panic shields, thread-local error storage
- **Python** (`ts-pack-python`): PyO3/maturin bindings with PyCapsule support, abi3 stable ABI
- **Node.js** (`ts-pack-node`): NAPI-RS bindings with TypeScript definitions
- **Go** (`ts-pack-go`): cgo bindings with platform-specific build directives (dev/prod/windows)
- **Java** (`ts-pack-java`): Panama FFM bindings (JDK 22+)
- **Elixir** (`ts-pack-elixir`): Rustler NIF bindings with ExUnit tests
- **CLI** (`ts-pack-cli`): clap-based grammar management tool
- **E2E generator**: Fixture-driven test generation for 7 language backends (21 fixtures)
- **Domain-split CI**: 8 independent workflows (validate, rust, python, node, go, java, elixir, c)
- **Publish pipeline**: Multi-registry publishing with pre-flight checks, Go FFI artifact distribution, GitHub Releases
- **CHANGELOG.md**: Full history from v0.1.0 through v1.0.0-rc.1

## Test plan

- [ ] `task lint` passes all pre-commit hooks
- [ ] `task rust:test:quick` passes Rust unit tests
- [ ] `task e2e:test:rust` passes Rust E2E tests
- [ ] `task go:test` passes Go unit tests with `-tags tspack_dev`
- [ ] CI workflows trigger correctly on push (path-based filtering)
- [ ] `publish.yaml` dry-run mode prepares artifacts without publishing
- [ ] Go FFI build produces `libts_pack_ffi.a` on linux/macos/windows

items.body:
## Summary

Consolidate CI from 5 jobs to 1 and make `bazelisk test ... --build_tests_only` the single source of truth for testing. Rewrite mock binaries in Python with unit tests so we're testing `.bzl` rule logic, not the OpenROAD binary.

**Before**: 5 CI jobs, ad-hoc shell scripts in `.github/scripts/`, nested `bazel` invocations inside tests that spawned extra servers and exhausted disk. **After**: one CI job, 20 test targets, 3s critical path (cached), zero nested bazel.

## Changes

- **Mock-openroad/klayout rewritten in Python** with 36 unit tests. Shell wrappers delegate to Python via runfiles. Shared `mock_helpers.py` loader avoids duplication.
- **Per-PDK `build_test` targets** for all 4 PDKs (asap7, nangate45, sky130hd, ihp-sg13g2) — real OpenROAD, full flow through `generate_abstract`.
- **CI consolidated to one job**: lint, lockfile check, `bazelisk test ... --build_tests_only`. The `--build_tests_only` flag prevents building non-test targets (e.g. heavyweight sram flows).
- **Disk cache GC** in `.bazelrc` (`--experimental_disk_cache_gc_max_size=5G`) to prevent CI runner disk exhaustion.
- **Deleted** `.github/scripts/` (smoketests.sh, build_local_target.sh, extract_docker_revision.py) and nested-bazel test scripts (open_target_test.sh, local_build_test.sh, genrule_config_test.sh, query_target_test.sh).
- **TESTING.md** with exhaustive table of all test targets, their mocking strategy, and PDK.

## Test plan

- `bazelisk test ... --build_tests_only --keep_going` passes all 20 tests
- `bazelisk build //... --nobuild` — 318 targets analyze clean
- No nested bazel invocations in any test
- Profile: 3s total, 438ms critical path (cached)

items.title: fix(java): rewrite literalEval() with recursive descent parser
items.body:
This PR fixes #228 related to the `literalEval()` implementation in `concoredocker.java`.

The previous implementation was using `String.split(",")`, which caused problems in several situations such as nested structures (like lists inside dictionaries), strings containing commas or colons, and cases with escape sequences. Because of this, some valid Python literals were not being parsed correctly.

To fix this, I replaced the logic with a recursive descent parser that can properly handle Python literal syntax. I also added an `escapePythonString()` method for correct string escaping and updated `toPythonLiteral()` so it uses Python values like `True`, `False`, and `None`.

Some additional fixes were also made:
- `read()` now returns `List<Object>` and includes a retry mechanism
- `write()` delay is changed to `double` instead of `int`, with separate catch blocks
- `simtime` type is updated to `double`

I also added a test file `TestLiteralEval.java` with 49 test cases to verify different scenarios.

All 49 tests pass locally.

This PR contains only the Java files as requested:
- `concoredocker.java` (fixed implementation)
- `TestLiteralEval.java` (test suite)

Fixes #228

items.title: 157 devops add smoke testing to ci-cd pipeline
items.body:
## Description
Adds smoke test for ci.yml (checks if the app is operational locally before new changes are merget).
Adds smoke test for cd.yml (checks if the prod-server is operational after deployment. Which means directly on the prod-server.


## Related Issue
Closes #157 

## Type of Change
- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Rewrite (Python → Ruby)
- [ ] Documentation
- [x] DevOps / Infrastructure
- [ ] Chore

## Changes Made
- Added smoke test job to CI pipeline that spins up the app locally and verifies HTTP 200 on localhost:4567
- Added smoke test job to CD pipeline that verifies HTTP 200 on the production server after deployment
- Both smoke tests run only if the preceding job succeeds (build-test / build-push)

## How to Test
1. Open a PR to main or push directly to main or development
2. Go to Actions tab in GitHub and observe the workflow runs
3. Verify that both smoke-test-ci and smoke-test-cd jobs pass with "✅ Smoke test passed"

## Checklist
- [ ] Tested locally
- [ ] OpenAPI spec updated (if endpoint changed)
- [x] No hardcoded secrets or credentials
- [ ] Database migrations work (if applicable)


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Tests**
  * Added CI and CD smoke tests that perform HTTP health checks and fail the pipeline if the app is unresponsive; CI also ensures services are torn down after testing.
* **Chores**
  * Minor workflow/comment formatting and newline fixes for more consistent tooling behavior.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

items.title: post-release.yml: auto-update Package.swift after release
items.body:
## What

Two coordinated workflow changes that close the Swift channel gap surfaced by #1062:

1. **`post-release.yml`** gains an `update-swift-package` job. On every release publish (or `workflow_dispatch` with a `tag` input), it polls for `chordsketch-xcframework.zip` on the release, computes its SHA256, rewrites `packages/swift/Package.swift`'s `binaryTarget` `url` and `checksum` fields, and opens a PR against `main`.
2. **`swift.yml`** gains a `workflow_dispatch` trigger with an optional `tag` input. When invoked with a tag, the build job checks out that tag and the publish job uploads the resulting xcframework to the matching release.

The Package.swift rewrite uses the same paren-counting parser as the existing in-CI rewrite in `swift.yml`'s test job, so URLs/checksums containing `)` do not fool the matcher.

## Why

#1062 documented two compounding problems:

- Package.swift on `main` had `checksum: "PLACEHOLDER"` and pointed at a v0.1.0 xcframework asset that does not exist on the release, breaking `swift package add https://github.com/koedame/chordsketch.git` for any tag.
- swift.yml was added **after** v0.1.0 was tagged. Its publish job is gated on `startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')`, so it never ran for v0.1.0; and even if it had, nothing rewrote Package.swift.

After this PR merges, the next release (v0.2.x or later) will automatically trigger the new `update-swift-package` job, which uploads a Package.swift PR that the human reviewer can squash-merge once CI is green.

The PR is opened via `CLAUDE_PAT` rather than the default `GITHUB_TOKEN` so that CI fires on it normally — bot-pushed PRs created with `GITHUB_TOKEN` silently do not trigger downstream workflows, which would block branch protection's required-checks gate.

## Required user action — v0.1.0 remediation (optional)

This PR fixes future releases automatically but does not retroactively fix v0.1.0. To make `swift package add ...@0.1.0` resolvable, run the following after this PR is merged:

1. **Build and upload v0.1.0 xcframework**:
   - GitHub Actions → "Swift Package" workflow → "Run workflow"
   - On the `main` branch, set `tag: v0.1.0`, click Run.
   - Wait for the `publish` job to finish; verify `chordsketch-xcframework.zip` is now on the v0.1.0 release page.

2. **Trigger Package.swift update for v0.1.0**:
   - GitHub Actions → "Post-Release Package Updates" workflow → "Run workflow"
   - On the `main` branch, set `tag: v0.1.0`, click Run.
   - The `update-swift-package` job will compute the checksum and open a PR.

3. **Merge the auto-generated PR**: it will be titled `Update Swift Package.swift to v0.1.0`. After CI is green, squash-merge it.

If you choose **not** to remediate v0.1.0, the Swift package will simply start working with v0.2.0+. Document this in the release notes.

## Test results

- `python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/post-release.yml'))"` — passes
- `python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/swift.yml'))"` — passes
- The Package.swift rewriting Python uses the same paren-counting algorithm as the existing in-CI rewrite at `swift.yml:111-172`, which is already exercised by every PR touching swift.yml.
- The new job has guards for: missing asset (polls 30 min then fails loudly), no-op re-run (skips push if Package.swift unchanged), and pre-existing branch on retry (deletes and recreates).

## Review summary

Self-review only. Awaiting auto-review.

Refs #1062 — keep open until v0.1.0 remediation decision is finalized.

Updates `org.jobrunr:jobrunr` from 8.4.2 to 8.5.0
Release notes

Sourced from org.jobrunr:jobrunr's releases.

v8.5.0

JobRunr 8.5.0 and JobRunr Pro 8.5.0

JobRunr 8.5.0 brings simplified Kotlin support, faster startup times, and several bug fixes. JobRunr Pro 8.5.0 adds two highly requested features: external jobs and dashboard audit logs.

Highlighted changes

Simplified Kotlin support

We've changed how Kotlin support packages are released. Instead of maintaining multiple modules with the Kotlin version baked into the artifact name (e.g. jobrunr-kotlin-2.0-support, jobrunr-kotlin-2.1-support), there is now a single jobrunr-kotlin-support artifact. This means less duplication on our side and a more predictable dependency for you. The package targets Kotlin 2.2 as its baseline, which JobRunr will stay on for the foreseeable future, but it also supports 2.1 and has already been tested against 2.3.

Note for JobRunr Pro: the packages are prefixed with jobrunr-pro giving jobrunr-pro-kotlin-support.

External Jobs (Pro)

An ExternalJob is a job that requires feedback from an external process to determine the success of the Job. This breaks the usual JobRunr flow where JobRunr marks a job as completed as soon as the method finishes.

This new feature is very handy for serverless computation (trigger a Lambda or Cloud Function and wait for a callback), long-running third-party workflows (AI pipeline, video transcoding, payment processing, ...), or human-in-the-loop approvals.

You can now create these jobs as follows:

First create the external job

JobProId jobId = BackgroundJob.create(anExternalJob()
                .withDetails(() -> nonBlockingRequest()));

Later signal the success (or failure or progress)

BackgroundJob.signalExternalJobSucceeded(jobId.asUUID());

You can also create and manage ExternalRecurringJobs.

Dashboard Audit Logging (Pro)

We now log actions performed by the dashboard user. You can expect to find messages like these in your logs:

INFO  o.j.dashboard.DashboardAuditLogger - User(id='user@jobrunr.io', username='user@jobrunr.io') accessed the server overview
INFO  o.j.dashboard.DashboardAuditLogger - User(id='user@jobrunr.io', username='user@jobrunr.io') accessed 6 job(s)
INFO  o.j.dashboard.DashboardAuditLogger - User(id='user@jobrunr.io', username='user@jobrunr.io') changed the state of 2 job(s)

With extra details logged at DEBUG level:

DEBUG o.j.dashboard.DashboardAuditLogger - User(id='user@jobrunr.io', username='user@jobrunr.io') accessed Job(id='019cb439-5093-7c7a-89a6-e05354c091f1', jobName='org.jobrunr.stubs.TestService.enqueueNewBatchWorkWithBuilder(3)')
</tr></table>

Release notes

Sourced from io.opentelemetry:opentelemetry-api's releases.

Version 1.60.1

This is a patch release on the previous 1.60.0 release, fixing the issue(s) below.

SDK

Extensions

  • Autoconfigure: fix warning always emitted (#8157)

Version 1.60.0

API

  • Support W3C trace context random flag (#8012)
  • Clarify that SpanBuilder.setAttribute allows null values (#8072)

Incubating

  • Implement environment variable context propagation carriers (#8074)
  • Deprecate ExtendedAttributes, ExtendedAttributeKey, ExtendedAttributeType, ExtendedAttributesBuilder (#8060)
  • Deprecate peerServiceMapping accessor from InstrumentationConfigUtil (#8088)

SDK

Traces

  • Disable warning when using TraceIdRatioBasedSampler as root sampler (#8065)
  • User-supplied attributes take precedence over exception-derived attributes (#7993)
  • Exclude META-INF/maven from traces SDK shaded dependencies (#8096)
  • Fix inverted condition in LegacySpanProcessorInstrumentation#finishSpans (#8145)

Metrics

  • Split out cumulative vs. delta storage (#8015)
  • Add metrics for PeriodicMetricReader (#8038)
  • Allow configuring min/max in histograms (#8095)
  • Remove deprecated otel.experimental.metrics.cardinality.limit property (#8124)
  • BREAKING bug fix: GlobUtil and IncludePatternMatching, used in views and declarative config matching, previously were inconsistent in case sensitivity. If a glob char (* or ?) was present, it was evaluated with case sensitivity. If no glob chars were present, it was evaluated with case insensitivity. Now, all are consistently evaluated with case sensitivity. (#8152)

Logs

  • Stabilize LogRecordBuilder.setException (#8089)
  • Clarify setTimestamp javadoc for event timestamp behavior (#8104)

Exporters

  • OTLP: Fix marshaling for empty string attributes (#8014)
  • Prometheus: Update Prometheus client to 1.5.0 (#8080)
  • OTLP: Serialize exponential histogram sum as optional double (#8107)
  • OTLP: Use KeyManagerFactory.getDefaultAlgorithm() in TlsUtil.keyManager() (#8122)
  • OTLP: Ensure proto serializer idCache is cleared when exception is thrown (#8147)
